questions.text: What is the central theme of Surah Yunus?
questions.answer: The truth of Revelation, the Oneness of Allah, and the reality of the Messengers, with a focus on Allah's Wisdom and Power in creation.

questions.text: What challenge does Allah give to those who say the Prophet fabricated the Quran (Verse 38)?
questions.answer: To bring forth just one Surah like it and call upon anyone they can besides Allah for help.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe the 'Awliya' (friends) of Allah in Verses 62-63?
questions.answer: They are those who believe and used to fear Allah (Taqwa); they shall have no fear, nor shall they grieve.

questions.text: What was the unique characteristic of the people of Prophet Yunus (AS) compared to other nations?
questions.answer: They were the only nation that believed as a whole when they saw the punishment coming, and Allah subsequently removed the punishment from them.

questions.text: What did Fir'aun (Pharaoh) say when he was about to drown?
questions.answer: He said, 'I believe that there is no deity except He in whom the Children of Israel believe, and I am of the Muslims'.

questions.text: What was Allah's response to Pharaoh's last-minute conversion?
questions.answer: It was rejected because he had been disobedient and a corrupter until that very moment, though his body was preserved as a sign.

questions.text: What does Verse 57 describe the Quran as for the hearts of people?
questions.answer: A healing (Shifa') for what is in the breasts, a guidance, and a mercy.

questions.text: Why are the disbelievers told to travel through the earth?
questions.answer: To see the end of those who came before them and rejected the Messengers.

questions.text: Does Allah force people to become believers according to Verse 99?
questions.answer: No; it states that if Allah had willed, everyone on earth would have believed, so the Prophet should not compel people to believe.

questions.text: What is the 'Boushra' (glad tidings) promised to the believers in this life and the hereafter?
questions.answer: It refers to the peace, divine support, and righteous dreams in this life, and the reward of Paradise in the next.
